RepresentativeInfo.CapitolAddress: MO House of Representatives, 201 West Capitol Avenue, Room 115-D, Jefferson City MO 65101
RepresentativeInfo.Biography:
Representative Carolyn Caton, a Republican, proudly represents part of Jackson County (District 33) in the Missouri House of Representatives. She was elected to her first two-year term in November 2024.
Born in Kansas City, Missouri, and residing most of her life in Blue Springs, Rep. Caton graduated from Blue Springs High School in 1984. She pursued higher education at Baker University, earning a Bachelor of Business Administration in 1996 and receiving Cum Laude honors. She later attained a Master of Science in Public Service Administration from the University of Evansville in 2004.
Rep. Caton has over 25 years of experience in the transportation industry, including roles in less-than-truckload (LTL), truckload (TL), and logistics. Her distinguished career includes being named Terminal Manager of the Year twice for a national LTL carrier, a testament to her leadership and problem-solving skills.
Beyond her professional achievements, Rep. Caton has been a dedicated advocate for her community. While serving as a park commissioner for Blue Springs, the park commission received an award for Outstanding Park Commission for a City of Under 50k Residents. Caton was also named Volunteer of the Year by the Jackson County Republican Party. She remains actively involved in civic and faith-based initiatives, serving as an elder and board member for First Christian Church in Blue Springs, a board member for the MLK Community Event, and an avid volunteer for numerous organizations.
Rep. Caton's extensive background in transportation and logistics equips her with critical insight into the state's challenges and opportunities for growth. Coupled with her leadership in public service, she is committed to fostering responsible governance, advocating for efficient transportation policies, and strengthening the well-being of her constituents.
Rep. Caton resides in Blue Springs with her husband, Rick, and continues to serve with integrity, dedication, and a passion for making a difference.

SponsoredBill.LongTitle: Authorizes the conveyance of certain state property located in Jackson County
Modifies provisions relating to the minimum wage, exempting employees under 20 years of age from minimum wage increases
Modifies provisions relating to employee compensation, repealing the minimum wage cost of living adjustment and modifying paid sick leave provisions
Authorizes the city of Lake Lotawana to levy a sales tax for public safety, upon voter approval
Authorizes an income tax deduction for certain election worker compensation
Establishes the "Contingency Management Program" within the department of corrections
Modifies provisions relating to property taxes
Proposes an amendment to the Constitution of Missouri to require Jackson County to have an elected assessor
Grants use of the House Chamber for the Women Legislators of Missouri DeVerne Lee Calloway Award Ceremony and Scholarship Presentation on Monday, April 7, 2025, from 1:00 p.m. to 3:00 p.m.

CoSponsoredBill.LongTitle: Establishes provisions relating to civil liability for publishing or distributing material harmful to minors on the internet
Modifies and establishes provisions relating to judicial proceedings
Prohibits state departments from spending money on diversity, equity, and inclusion initiatives
Modifies provisions relating to the state tax commission's equalization authority and enforcement authority, authorizing withholding of certain revenues as a result of noncompliance
Designates the last week in April each year as "Infertility Awareness Week"
Proposes a constitutional amendment relating to real property tax assessments
